postText: How will #solar module costs evolve by 2030? @IRENA analysis compares different solar technologies & how sustainability factors shape production costs over time. See more: https://t.co/a0LXwKTSOy https://t.co/zSOFqRgB0x
bundle.chartTitle: Cost tool for the total module production cost (TOPCon & Monocrystalline)
keyFindings: The total module production cost for all four categories is projected to decline steadily between 2025 and 2030.
Incorporating ESG (environmental, social, and governance) factors adds a consistent cost premium of approximately 0.002 to 0.004 USD/Wp for both Monocrystalline and TOPCon modules.
In 2025, TOPCon (with ESG) has the highest projected cost at nearly 0.180 USD/Wp, while Monocrystalline (without ESG) is the lowest at roughly 0.174 USD/Wp.
By 2030, TOPCon (without ESG) is expected to reach a lower cost point (approx. 0.167 USD/Wp) than Monocrystalline (with ESG), which stays near 0.173 USD/Wp.
Monocrystalline technology without ESG remains the lowest cost option throughout the projection period, reaching approximately 0.169 USD/Wp by 2030.

sourceLines.sourceText: Notes: ESG = environmental, social and governance; USD = United States Dollar; Wp = watt peak
